Norwegian Energy Company ASA ( (GB:0HTF) ) has shared an announcement.

BlueNord ASA has called its annual general meeting for 18 May 2026 at 16:00 CEST, opting to hold the session entirely as a digital event with no physical shareholder attendance. Shareholders are directed to the company’s website for login instructions and the documents to be considered, reflecting an ongoing shift toward virtual governance practices and compliance with Norwegian securities disclosure rules.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:0HTF) stock is a Buy with a NOK570.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Norwegian Energy Company ASA stock, see the GB:0HTF Stock Forecast page.

More about Norwegian Energy Company ASA

BlueNord ASA is a European oil and gas company focused on producing and developing energy resources while supporting the broader energy transition. The company holds a 36.8 percent stake in the Danish Underground Consortium, which operates oil and gas fields in the Danish North Sea, and its shares are listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange under the ticker BNOR.

data.title: Vitec Software Group Lifts Margins and Expands Portfolio in Strong First Quarter
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Vitec Software Group AB Class B ( ($SE:VIT.B) ) has shared an update.

Vitec Software Group reported a solid start to 2026, with first-quarter net sales rising 9% to SEK 955 million and recurring revenues up 7% to SEK 839 million, driven by both innovation-led upselling and price adjustments. Profitability improved as EBITA grew 11% to SEK 244 million, lifting the EBITA margin to 26%, while earnings per share before dilution increased 19%, despite a roughly 3% negative currency impact.

The company completed the acquisitions of Dutch firm Autonet and Swedish firm Infometric, adding over 75 employees and reinforcing its vertical software portfolio and recurring revenue base. Strong cash flow from operating activities of SEK 784 million and stable net debt at 2.0 times EBITDA, supported by an oversubscribed SEK 700 million bond issue, leave Vitec well positioned for further M&A and continued investment in AI-enhanced, mission-critical products across the group.

The most recent analyst rating on ($SE:VIT.B) stock is a Hold with a SEK410.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Vitec Software Group AB Class B stock, see the SE:VIT.B Stock Forecast page.

More about Vitec Software Group AB Class B

Vitec Software Group AB is a vertical market software company that develops and delivers mission-critical solutions to niche industries. The group focuses on subscription-based and transaction-based software, serving around 27,000 customers in 13 countries with 49 business units and a high share of recurring revenues.

Average Trading Volume: 158,916

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: SEK11.12B

An update from VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund Limited AccumUSD ( (GB:VOF) ) is now available.

VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund Limited has repurchased 16,999 US dollar-denominated ordinary shares at GBP 4.691313 per share, which will be held in treasury, as part of its ongoing capital management activities. Following the transaction, the fund holds 6,573,020 treasury shares, with 123,336,639 shares in issue and voting rights outstanding, a figure investors will use to assess disclosure thresholds under UK transparency rules.

The updated voting rights total of 123,336,639 defines the new denominator for shareholders when calculating whether they must report holdings or changes in holdings under FCA disclosure guidance. This adjustment may subtly influence liquidity and ownership reporting dynamics for investors in the Guernsey-based Vietnam-focused fund, while signalling continued active balance sheet management by the company.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:VOF) stock is a Buy with a £5.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund Limited AccumUSD stock, see the GB:VOF Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, VOF is a Neutral.

The score is primarily supported by the company’s low-leverage balance sheet and strong cash generation, which reduce financial risk. This is tempered by highly volatile profitability (recent swing to losses), mixed technical momentum, and a negative P/E that limits valuation support.

To see Spark’s full report on VOF stock, click here.

More about VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund Limited AccumUSD

The fund focuses on Vietnam-related investment opportunities, giving shareholders exposure to the Vietnamese market through a regulated collective investment scheme structure.

St. James’s Place ( (GB:STJ) ) has provided an announcement.

St. James’s Place has continued its share buyback activity, confirming that it repurchased 231,044 ordinary shares of 15 pence each on 22 April 2026 via Morgan Stanley on the London Stock Exchange and various multilateral trading facilities, at an average price of 1,298.4521 pence. The company plans to cancel these shares, reducing the total number of shares in issue to 519,398,538, a move that marginally enhances earnings per share and may support the stock’s valuation while providing an updated denominator for investors’ disclosure and transparency calculations.

The reduction in share capital through cancellation underscores management’s ongoing capital-return programme, signalling confidence in the firm’s balance sheet and future cash generation. For shareholders and other market participants, the new share count becomes the key reference point for assessing ownership stakes, regulatory reporting thresholds and potential dilution or concentration of holdings over time.

The latest announcement is out from Prudential ( (GB:PRU) ).

Prudential plc has repurchased 277,659 of its ordinary shares from JP Morgan Securities on 22 April 2026 at an average price of £11.2657 per share, with prices ranging between £11.2350 and £11.3050. The transaction was executed on the London Stock Exchange under UK and Hong Kong rules and forms part of a shareholder-approved buyback arrangement with JP Morgan.

The company intends to cancel the repurchased shares, reducing the number of shares in issue to 2,522,727,996 and aligning the total voting rights with this figure. This buyback and cancellation slightly enhances remaining shareholders’ proportional ownership and may support capital management objectives and trading liquidity across Prudential’s multiple listing venues.

data.title: RWS posts solid first-half growth as AI translation push gathers pace
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

RWS Holdings ( (GB:RWS) ) has provided an update.

RWS reported first-half revenue of about £360m, up around 5% year on year, with organic constant-currency growth of roughly 7% driven by strong gains in its Generate and Protect segments as it pivots Transform to technology-first solutions. Adjusted profit before tax rose to approximately £24m from £18m, supported by an efficiency programme, while net debt stood at about £33m after paying a £17m final dividend, with management saying momentum and client wins underpin expectations for full-year results in line with guidance.

The Group highlighted particularly robust double-digit growth in its TrainAI unit and launched Language Weaver Pro, a new AI translation model developed with Cohere that it says outperforms leading rivals on benchmark tests, underscoring its push to lead in enterprise translation technology. Alongside new AI patents, a client-validated platform proof-of-concept in Transform and ongoing process rationalisation, these initiatives are presented as key to reinforcing RWS’s position as the “cultural intelligence layer” for enterprise AI and securing improving profitability and cash generation in FY26.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:RWS) stock is a Buy with a £175.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on RWS Holdings stock, see the GB:RWS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on RWS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RWS is a Neutral.

RWS Holdings’ stock score is primarily driven by its strong technical indicators, suggesting bullish momentum. However, financial performance challenges and a negative P/E ratio weigh down the score. The high dividend yield is a positive factor for income investors.

To see Spark’s full report on RWS stock, click here.

More about RWS Holdings

RWS Holdings is a UK-headquartered global AI solutions company focused on making enterprise AI culturally fluent, contextually accurate and secure. Through its Generate, Transform and Protect segments, it delivers intelligent content, enterprise knowledge, large-scale localisation and IP protection, serving more than 80 of the world’s top 100 brands and operating with a proprietary Cultural Intelligence Layer backed by over 45 patents.

data.title: Digitalbox non-executive director Claire Blunt to step down from board
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Digitalbox plc ( (GB:DBOX) ) is now available.

Digitalbox plc has announced that non-executive director Claire Blunt will resign and leave the board at the end of April 2026 to concentrate on her executive role as CFO and COO of Steven.com. Blunt, who joined the board in October 2024 and chaired the remuneration committee, is departing with warm tributes from chairman Marcus Rich and has in turn praised the board and management team, signalling an orderly governance transition rather than any strategic shift.

The change removes an experienced non-executive from Digitalbox’s boardroom, but the cordial statements from both sides suggest continuity in the company’s oversight and strategy across its mobile-first portfolio of entertainment and youth-focused digital brands. For stakeholders, the move underlines the increasing demands on senior executives holding multiple roles, while indicating that Digitalbox’s day-to-day operations and growth plans remain unaffected by this board-level change.

Spark’s Take on DBOX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DBOX is a Neutral.

The score is primarily driven by mixed financial performance: a strong balance sheet and improved revenue momentum are offset by ongoing losses and volatile free cash flow. Technically, the stock shows a weaker medium-to-long trend with neutral near-term momentum. Valuation cannot be supported from the provided P/E and missing dividend yield, which weighs on the overall score.

To see Spark’s full report on DBOX stock, click here.

More about Digitalbox plc

Digitalbox plc is a UK-based, mobile-first digital media company focused on delivering profitable publishing at scale through digital advertising. It owns a portfolio of entertainment, satire and youth-culture brands including Entertainment Daily, The Daily Mash, The Tab, The Poke, TV Guide and several TV soap and Royal Family-focused Insider sites.

Average Trading Volume: 57,662

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: £5.13M
